section: to put it into words

lyrics

This is where you will find the lyrics for the words I sing.

home bio thoughts songs lyrics cool music
welcome to the thumpi home page • blue to the pop • more updates to the site soon • welcome to the thumpi home page • blue to the pop • more updates to the site soon • welcome to the thumpi home page • blue to the pop • more updates to the site soon • welcome to the thumpi home page • blue to the pop • more updates to the site soon •

lyrics

Curtain Calls Don't Applaud Themselves

Call this romance a dress rehearsal
If the audience never shows

You look good in anything, even the air
Your palms tell me a tale too good to be true
You love all of my jokes
But you smile like you’ve heard the punchline, before I fuck it up

You lack a habit of seeing my flaws for what they are
You've got a mouth but you are unable to scream

You say "I love you" sotto voce, I'd yell it back if I could
I'd give everything to hear you sing
If I were a real man I'd see this for what it is

Your coat still claims the back of my chair, like it paid rent to be there
My friends ask I dodge the questions
You never fight, you never flinch, so what do they need to know?

You say "I love you" sotto voce, I'd yell it back if I could
I swear you’re dancing down the hallway
But I never hear the floorboards creak
I'd give everything to hear you sing
If I were a real man I'd see this for what it is

You're so out of touch, I'm so out of line
You're so hard to reach, I can't handle basic speech
You're so real to me, to be or not to be

I would pay you to tell me, “shut your stupid mouth”
’Cause anything thing you say is as soothing as the rain
But you just smile and let me ramble
You say "I love you" sotto voce, I'd yell it back if I could

So take a rest inside my head, where the lighting's always right

Letters I Never Sent

I've written this a thousand times before but never like this
I'd like to think I'd be famous for all the letters I've kept

Sometimes I idolise the life that could've been
But then it feels so dumb to think "what if"
So then I just go back to sleep

But by the morning, I find myself writing another one
By the evening I find it futile again and throw it all away

I could tell you how I feel but I'd rather you never know
I don't wanna die alone but I'd rather turn off my phone

I've never been flooded by calls and it's not gonna start now
Feels like it doesn't matter how hard I try
'Cause I'm just not the good guy

"Blah, blah," you say it like it's easy
Like anyone could get it, could do it
I don't want any of your fucking white lies
none of your sympathy advice

By the morning I find myself writing another one
By the evening I find it futile again and throw it all away

I could tell you how I feel but I'd rather you never know
I don't wanna die alone but I'd rather turn off my phone
I could tell you how I feel but I'd rather you never know
I don't wanna die alone but I'd rather turn off my phone
should be viewed on a computer unless you are a dweeb • no trackers • no bullshit • made with love :)
